jokes.text: The cowboy hat with that bolo string, what is that?
jokes.setup: Larry describing why he didn't help someone pick up a golf ball
jokes.punchline: His visceral reaction to a fashion accessory as justification for rudeness
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry David neurosis - making a superficial judgment about someone's appearance justify antisocial behavior. Good character integration.

jokes.text: I see certain items, and I recoil in horror.
jokes.setup: Continuing explanation about the bolo tie
jokes.punchline: The dramatic overstatement of 'recoil in horror' for a clothing item
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David language - the melodramatic 'recoil in horror' applied to something trivial. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: He might have to have bypass surgery. - Really? Can you believe that? - No.
jokes.setup: Serious medical news about Jeff
jokes.punchline: Larry's flat 'No' response to major surgery news
jokes.explanation: Mild understatement humor, functional but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: How are you, Gil? You sounded like my friend John.
jokes.setup: Larry answers phone thinking it's John
jokes.punchline: Mistaking Gil for John sets up the whole episode's confusion
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection that establishes the episode's premise but isn't particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: He used to be a porno actor like 15 years ago. No, I don't wanna go to a porn party.
jokes.setup: Larry reveals Gil's past profession
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's immediate assumption about what kind of party it will be
jokes.explanation: Good setup-punchline with Cheryl's reasonable but wrong assumption creating the humor.

jokes.text: I need you to get them out of there... if something happens to me
jokes.setup: Jeff describes elaborate porn hiding scheme
jokes.punchline: The absurd lengths he's gone to hide porn from his wife
jokes.explanation: Strong cringe comedy - the elaborate secret compartment for porn is both pathetic and relatable for many men.

jokes.text: That's your own deal, Repression Jones.
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining his porn anxiety
jokes.punchline: Larry's dismissive nickname combining psychology with a generic surname
jokes.explanation: 'Repression Jones' is a perfect Larry David coinage - clever, dismissive, and highly quotable. Great economy of language.

jokes.text: What if I get in an accident on the way home? The porn flies everywhere, strewn all over the car, all over my bleeding body.
jokes.setup: Larry considering what could go wrong with the porn mission
jokes.punchline: The vivid image of porn scattered around a car accident scene
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation and vivid imagery. The phrase 'all over my bleeding body' elevates this from simple worry to absurd nightmare scenario. Classic Larry catastrophizing.

jokes.text: Try not to die. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Larry leaving Jeff before surgery
jokes.punchline: Casual goodbye for life-threatening surgery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry sendoff - treating major surgery like a casual errand. Great economy and very quotable.

jokes.text: Then why did you get it? I don't know. I wanted to get everything. I wanted to have all the loaded things.
jokes.setup: Larry admits he doesn't know how to use his navigation system
jokes.punchline: His childish reasoning for buying technology he can't use
jokes.explanation: 'All the loaded things' is peak Larry - wanting status symbols without understanding their purpose. Relatable male behavior.

jokes.text: When were you gonna learn? I don't know. I thought somebody would teach me.
jokes.setup: Cheryl pressing Larry about learning the navigation system
jokes.punchline: Larry's passive assumption that someone else would handle it
jokes.explanation: Larry's learned helplessness with technology - expecting others to solve his self-created problems.

jokes.text: You're lost? I wasn't lost then. You didn't know where you were going. In my book, that's lost.
jokes.setup: Stranger questioning Larry's navigation
jokes.punchline: The logical dismantling of Larry's denial about being lost
jokes.explanation: Good logic-based humor - the stranger's reasonable definition of 'lost' versus Larry's technical denial.

jokes.text: There's no hostility, there's no anger. Can I just have the paper back? Please! You snap!
jokes.setup: Larry claiming he's not angry while clearly getting frustrated
jokes.punchline: His immediate explosion right after denying hostility
jokes.explanation: Classic ironic escalation - Larry proves the stranger right about his anger immediately after denying it.

jokes.text: I hope you stay lost!
jokes.setup: The helpful stranger has been pushed too far
jokes.punchline: His petty curse as final word in the argument
jokes.explanation: Perfect petty revenge line - the stranger's final 'curse' is both childish and satisfying.

jokes.text: I've always wanted to drive around in this area for hours. It's quite scenic, don't you think? And they have some very colorful characters. I enjoyed Baby Jane a lot, didn't you?
jokes.setup: Larry and Cheryl are completely lost
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic commentary on their miserable situation
jokes.explanation: Larry's defensive sarcasm in the face of Cheryl's anger. 'Baby Jane' is a good callback to the strange woman.

jokes.text: You wouldn't help me with the ball. The ball? The range ball. It fell and you wouldn't help me out.
jokes.setup: The guy from the driving range recognizes Larry
jokes.punchline: Bringing back the golf ball incident that opened the episode
jokes.explanation: Good callback that brings the episode full circle. The coincidence of meeting this guy again is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Maybe next time you'll remember to pick up the fucking golf ball.
jokes.setup: The insult about bending over
jokes.punchline: The aggressive lesson-teaching about golf etiquette
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the callback - the guy's petty revenge for Larry's rudeness at the driving range.

jokes.text: Jake's Way is not marked, there's no sign for it. You may have included that in the directions.
jokes.setup: Larry finally arrives at Gil's party after getting lost
jokes.punchline: His passive-aggressive criticism of Gil's directions
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - immediately blaming the host for his own navigation failures with understated sarcasm.

jokes.text: If you would take your shoes off... Really? Just take them off. It's just a custom my wife started in the house.
jokes.setup: Gil asks Larry to remove his shoes
jokes.punchline: Larry's disbelief at this simple house rule
jokes.explanation: Sets up Larry's shoe conflict that will explode later. His incredulity at basic politeness is very in-character.

jokes.text: I'll leave mine on, is that okay? They're completely clean. Look, see?
jokes.setup: Gil insists on the no-shoes rule
jokes.punchline: Larry trying to negotiate and showing his shoe bottoms
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry behavior - trying to lawyer his way out of a simple social courtesy. The visual of showing shoe bottoms is cringe gold.

jokes.text: Did he tell you this was a dinner party, Larry? No, he said 'party.' I wouldn't go to a dinner party.
jokes.setup: They discover it's a dinner party, not just a party
jokes.punchline: Larry's stubborn distinction between party types
jokes.explanation: Larry's arbitrary social distinctions - as if 'party' and 'dinner party' are completely different species of event.

jokes.text: Great place, my wife jokingly refers to it as 'the house that cum built.' 'Cause of my porns.
jokes.setup: Gil giving a house tour
jokes.punchline: Crude pun on 'the house that Jack built' relating to his porn career
jokes.explanation: Strong wordplay that's both clever and deeply inappropriate for the context. Very quotable and establishes Gil's character.

jokes.text: That's a collection of small bottles, and I like them. It's very interesting. They're not as big as normal.
jokes.setup: Gil showing off his collection
jokes.punchline: The pointless observation about bottle size
jokes.explanation: Absurd small talk - the complete obviousness of 'small bottles...not as big as normal' is wonderfully stupid.

jokes.text: We were so hungry, that we had a bag of potato chips in the car... Potato chips on the way to a dinner party? What's that?
jokes.setup: Larry explaining why they're not hungry
jokes.punchline: His social faux pas of admitting to eating before a dinner party
jokes.explanation: Larry's complete obliviousness to dinner party etiquette - admitting to eating beforehand is a major social error.

jokes.text: You know what Tabasco's good for? Keep you hard. Really? Only you gotta stick it up your ass.
jokes.setup: Gil teasing a porn story
jokes.punchline: The crude revelation about Tabasco sauce
jokes.explanation: Shocking setup-punchline that perfectly captures the inappropriate dinner conversation. Original and memorable.

jokes.text: He runs over to the craft service table, he comes back, his finger is red. Sticks it up my ass. Tabasco sauce!
jokes.setup: Gil's elaborate porn story building to climax
jokes.punchline: The reveal of the Tabasco solution
jokes.explanation: Excellent storytelling structure with perfect timing. The visual progression and punch of 'Tabasco sauce!' is expertly delivered.

jokes.text: I wasn't the biggest, all right? But I could stay hard the longest.
jokes.setup: Gil explaining his porn career advantages
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact assessment of his professional skills
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Gil's professional pride in his porn performance. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Maybe we can stop and pick up some Tabasco.
jokes.setup: Larry and Cheryl leaving the dinner party
jokes.punchline: Larry's dry reference to Gil's story
jokes.explanation: Great callback with perfect Larry deadpan delivery. The economy of the reference is excellent.

jokes.text: My back. Oh, shit! I'm so sorry! Everyone sit down. There's glass on the floor. No one move.
jokes.setup: Larry's back problems causing him to knock something over
jokes.punchline: The chaos and his panicked damage control
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy callback to the back excuse. Larry's panicked reaction to breaking something escalates the dinner party disaster.

jokes.text: It doesn't affect you, does it, the glass on the floor? Because you're wearing your fucking shoes in my house!
jokes.setup: Larry breaks a lamp while wearing shoes
jokes.punchline: Gil's explosive realization that the shoes actually protected Larry
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic payoff - the shoe rule Gil insisted on backfires perfectly. Larry's rudeness accidentally protects him from consequences.

jokes.text: My feet have a tendency to get a little chilly when I take my...
jokes.setup: Gil screaming about shoes
jokes.punchline: Larry's completely inappropriate attempt at reasonable explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry response - trying to have a rational conversation during someone's psychotic break. The polite tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: What's the level of anger here? What am I dealing with? I'd say... at least an 8.7.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to assess Cheryl's anger after the disaster
jokes.punchline: The precise numerical rating of anger
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry moment - treating human emotion like a technical measurement. The specificity of '8.7' is perfectly absurd and very quotable.

jokes.text: Then you broke that lamp... and that crazy woman screamed at you, and it got you some pity points. Pity points? Fabulous! I love pity points.
jokes.setup: Cheryl explaining how Larry's anger rating improved
jokes.punchline: Larry's genuine enthusiasm for earning 'pity points'
jokes.explanation: 'Pity points' is a brilliant Larry concept - gamifying marital conflict. His excitement about this system is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I don't wanna do a double-goodbye. I've already said goodbye under the worst circumstances. You want me to go and do the double-goodbye?
jokes.setup: Larry resisting going back for the watch
jokes.punchline: His specific horror of the 'double-goodbye' social situation
jokes.explanation: 'Double-goodbye' is a perfect Larry David invention - identifying a specific social awkwardness most people feel but never name. Highly quotable and relatable.

jokes.text: I gotta take my shoes off now.
jokes.setup: Larry forced to return to Gil's house
jokes.punchline: His resigned acceptance of having to follow the shoe rule again
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the shoe conflict. Larry's defeated tone shows he's learned nothing but must comply. Great economy.

jokes.text: Is that a barn? Think it was too small for a barn. It looked more like a stable.
jokes.setup: They're following directions to find 'the barn'
jokes.punchline: Their pedantic debate about barn vs. stable classification
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry and Cheryl overthinking simple directions. Their pedantic debate about agricultural buildings is very them.

jokes.text: Go up to my bedroom. To the left of the TV, there's a cabinet by the bookcase there. Open it up, move the linens, move 'em to the side, push on the back door. Inside there, I have my porn collection.
jokes.setup: Larry following Jeff's elaborate instructions
jokes.punchline: The absurd complexity of the porn hiding system being executed
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the ridiculous complexity of Jeff's system in action. The elaborate instructions being followed creates visual comedy.

jokes.text: Who is it? - Hotel security, ma'am, open up.
jokes.setup: Larry breaking into Jeff's house triggers alarm, cuts to apparent hotel scene
jokes.punchline: Reveals this is actually a porn video playing
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - audience thinks it's a real scene until context reveals it's porn dialogue. Classic bait and switch.

jokes.text: There's a prowler outside. I'm really scared. I can hear noises.
jokes.setup: Susie calls hotel security about a prowler
jokes.punchline: The 'prowler' is Larry following Jeff's instructions
jokes.explanation: Good situational irony - Susie's reasonable fear creates the exact scenario Jeff was trying to avoid. Setup for the porn discovery.

jokes.text: Hi. That's a friend of mine. Gil Thilander. I had dinner at his house... yesterday. You like that?
jokes.setup: Larry caught with porn, tries to explain
jokes.punchline: His pathetic attempt to contextualize the porn with Gil's story
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that brings the episode full circle. Larry's mortification and desperate explanation tie together multiple plot threads brilliantly.
